title: Example color scheme with Maroon, yellow and green
content:
Acorn Studio
Work Pricing About
Palette preview website

Design with warm contrast and soft natural tones

This demo uses your deep red, mustard, and green palette in a full landing page so you can judge the mood, readability, and balance of the colors in a realistic layout.

Warm and earthy • Distinct accent colors • Good for editorial or artisan brands
Seasonal collection

Muted warmth with organic accents and soft UI depth.

Editorial Warm Natural
Color balance
Looks especially nice for:

food brands, craft products, blogs, eco startups, and boutique shops.

Feature cards

This section helps you judge whether the palette works for content-heavy UI cards and icon accents.

A

Bold headline color

The deep red works well for strong typography and gives the page a slightly premium, earthy feel.

B

Warm backgrounds

The mustard shades are good as soft backgrounds, highlights, or subtle branding surfaces.

C

Healthy accents

The green shades give a natural accent that pairs nicely with the warmer red and yellow tones.

Overall impression

The palette feels warm, organic, and distinctive — not corporate blue, more handcrafted and welcoming.

 * Addicted: DreamLands
   * Effect: If he goes without experiencing the Dreamlands long enough to experience at least 1 Gales check for more than a night, he suffers the Deprived condition (-1 to Resistance Dicepools)
   * Beat: Ignore an obligation to get a fix, or get a fix after suffering from Deprivation
   * Resolution: Gain/Lose Belief or ES a Compromise.
--Other--
----------------------------------------
-Aspirations-
 * Become Soulshocked.
 * Use Second Glance wrongness to overcome a supernatural effect or the Gales.
 * To find schooling for the talking Kitten, Mattie.
-Vocation-
 * To explore the Dreamlands and learn more about how they function and can help people.
 * To defend someone else from an inhuman creature either physically or supernaturally.
 * The investigation of paranormal activity and protection of innocents.
----------------------------------------
-Breaking Points-
--Worst thing ever done?--
 * Eat food from a trash merely out of fear it go to waste in a time of mental fatigue.
--Worst imagined act (self?)--
 * Judging who gets to live or die, either for their 'sins' in a seat of power, or in an infamous "condemn a few to die so many can live" scenario.
--Worst imagined act (others)--
 * Disillusioned mass murder or abuse over a broken belief/agenda. While he can understand a flawed motive for even the most heinous of crimes (a want of power, belief, etc) a near or truly motiveless crime or one with virtually no (even perceived) gain by it's actor would be hard for him to rationalize.
--What's been forgotten?--
 * A creature, perhaps fey or dream lander, that was meant to protect a gateway of some kind that Drake stumbled across as a child. Whether this was a gate to the hedge, some dream realm, or somewhere between has long sense been lost by memory. Normally the guardian would go unnoticed, but unfortunately he was there at the right time to see said creature become captured, and supernaturally twisted in to some horrible beast against it's own better nature as a shadowy being took on the original guardian's appearance as if stolen, ready to lay in wait like a spider with the face of a godmother waiting for it's prey. He ran, deathly afraid, and was haunted by what he saw... and later realization that others may have fallen for that trap, with him able to do nothing about it. Eventually, it was mostly forgotten all together save for a feeling of regret and a need to do better.
--Most traumatic thing?--
 * Severe child abuse and near starvation that lead to a very young(age 6~7) near-suicide. Both the crux of his life-long Vocation and occasional rare bouts of Fugue/Dissociation when he is too deeply reminded of the negative aspects of his childhood.

-Description-
Country: USA       Age:    27 (14 Nov)
Sex:     Male      Race:   Caucasian
Hair:    Blond     Eyes:   Blue, sometimes Greyish or Greenish
Height:  69.5"     Weight: 190 lbs
Build:   Broad     Mood:   Tired but Attentive.
-----------------------------------------
-Appearance-
Drake has a rather young looking face, though it often rests in a monotone, almost aloof expression. When around those who have managed to befriend him, or uncommonly on exceptionally good days this can turn in to a smirk and a normally quiet demeanor opens up in to one that is prone to rambling on about any number of topics, or listening to another do the same. His hair is noticeably thinning at his relatively young age, much to his chagrin if noticed, and often left somewhat unkept even when in uniform. He is rarely fully shaven, though also never with a proper beard, passing by with a stubble even in uniform which he swears he has the "waiver" for. While off work, he often wears very casual and worn down clothing either in neutral or cooler colours, often with various internet or volunteer organization slogans. He hides his thinning hair usually under a bandana, or in a sillier mindset may even tie it in to a headband. In colder weather, he tends towards wearing one of his many collected hoodies, from various wolf-designs to "The Hoodie of Grey Tomorrow", Hoodies from the Squadrons he's served in, or his signature Wolf-patterned Fleece coat.
-----------------------------------------
-History-
As a very young child, Drake was raised alongside his brother(born female) by his father and a step mother. Unfortunately the latter took poorly to the two children when their father was away, and his early years were filled with life-threatening abuse and near-starvation. Even when eventually pulled away by child services, his mother found herself working 80+ hours a week to keep herself and children alive and healthy, and her vices didn't make this any easier.

With the shadows of his brother's pain and his own, the dream world became an escape. His step mother would often remind him how lucky he was to have a roof, or anything at all. However, seeing both the worst and best of people in the dreamlands, despite his abuse he turned her words in to his mission. If others were worse than himself, and even he suffered... rather than give up, he could live his life to see the world do better. As he helped and was helped in his dreams, he wished to take those dreams in to reality. With a younger brother to protect and an ideal growing in his heart, he began to overcome his life of shadows. His is curiosity of those unusually vivid dreams never did die even as they became rarer, leaving a sense of longing, but his smoldering light was now tempered with an idealistic purpose.

As he came of age in to his adulthood, he had mostly recovered from his trails of his very young age. His older childhood was rough, but it wasn't abusive. He had made a few close friends, and he had even taken up a job to help support his mother and brother. But he ran in to yet another road block in his high ideals of a better world... There was no way his family could afford college. Relationship hardship and the burden of caring for his family meant he never had time to excel in school for a scholarship. Still, he persistent held on.. burning slowly day by day as he found odd temp jobs to get by, saving money slowly. It was in the following years Drake and one of his closest friends, John, sought to join the military to seek college and maybe make the world better from within the political system. John was always the more politically savvy one, and more physically fit as well, but Drake was held back by his own fears of ever harming another for his own gain and at the time poor health. Once more, his persistence saw him keep his dream alive, training, studying, but it was his inspiring friend that kicked him with one last ultimatum. He wouldn't be young forever, and this door would close sooner than it would open. He needed to either join or find a new path, and Drake held back no further.

Despite tarnishing and smoldering, his bright heart and mind began to excel in his new job. His kind almost naive nature was always ready to help any of his colleagues in need. This coupled with a work ethic hardened by his younger life meant that while he was not always the most skilled at his job, he became quickly relied on as an encouraging leader on the working floor. Not in charge, but inspiring others to their own bests, and the willingness to face those in charge if he was in the right to do so.

title: Schutzrechte, Games, Software, Hardware und Patente – Forderungen für einen fairen Rechtsrahmen
content:
Schutzrechte, Games, Software, Hardware und Patente – Forderungen für einen fairen Rechtsrahmen

1. Grundsatz

Schutzrechte dürfen nicht isoliert betrachtet werden. Es reicht nicht, dass ein Unternehmen formal Rechte an Figuren, Marken, Software, Hardware, Plattformen oder Patenten besitzt. Entscheidend muss auch sein, ob diese Rechte fair, transparent und im Sinne der Gaming- und Nutzercommunity ausgeübt werden.

Ich bin nicht dafür, Schutz für konkrete Figuren, Namen, Designs, Musik, Marken, Software, Hardware oder Werke grundsätzlich abzuschaffen. Aber starker Schutz und harte rechtliche Durchsetzung sollten nur dann voll greifen, wenn Rechteinhaber auch klare Pflichten erfüllen.

Der rechtliche Rahmen müsste daher gestuft sein: Wer fair handelt, Kultur erhält, Technik zugänglich hält und Nutzerrechte respektiert, kann stärkere Schutzrechte beanspruchen. Wer dagegen Reihen vernachlässigt, Inhalte verschwinden lässt, Reparatur verhindert, Modding bekämpft, Patente breit auslegt oder nur mit Klagen und Kontrolle arbeitet, sollte nicht dieselbe starke Rechtsposition haben.

2. Schutz von Figuren, Marken und Werken

Konkrete Figuren, Namen, Marken, Designs, Musik, Assets und Werke können geschützt werden. Dieser Schutz darf aber nicht grenzenlos sein.

- Schutz konkreter Werke ja.
- Keine automatische Maximaldurchsetzung nur wegen Firmenbesitz.
- Schutz nur bei fairer Pflege, Verfügbarkeit und kultureller Verantwortung.
- Kein Missbrauch von Markenrechten gegen harmlose Fanarbeit, Archivierung oder Erhalt.
- Reihen mit großer kultureller Bedeutung müssen gepflegt und zugänglich gehalten werden.
- Wer eine Marke kontrolliert, muss auch Verantwortung für Qualität, Erhalt und faire Verfügbarkeit tragen.

3. Patente und Spielmechaniken

Patente dürfen nicht dazu dienen, naheliegende Spielideen, Genres oder technische Grundlogiken einzusperren. Gerade bei Games entstehen viele Mechaniken durch naheliegende Kombinationen bekannter Systeme.

- Keine breiten Patente auf normale Gameplay-Mechaniken.
- Keine Monopole auf Genres, Spielgefühle oder Grundideen.
- Keine Patentdrohungen gegen geistige Nachfolger oder kleinere Studios.
- Strenge Prüfung, ob wirklich eine technische Erfindung vorliegt.
- Keine Blockade von Kreativität durch allgemeine oder naheliegende Patentansprüche.

4. DRM, Online-Zwang und Serverabhängigkeit

Käufer müssen vor dem Kauf klar erkennen können, welche technischen Abhängigkeiten ein Spiel oder eine Software hat.

- DRM muss klar gekennzeichnet werden.
- Online-Zwang muss klar gekennzeichnet werden.
- Account-Zwang muss klar gekennzeichnet werden.
- Launcher-Zwang muss klar gekennzeichnet werden.
- Serverpflicht und Anti-Cheat-Eingriffe müssen klar erkennbar sein.
- DRM darf ein Spiel nach Supportende nicht zerstören.
- Aktivierungsserver müssen ersetzbar, abschaltbar oder langfristig gesichert werden.

5. Server-, Store- und Support-Ende

Wenn Spiele, Software oder Plattformen verkauft wurden, dürfen sie nicht einfach verschwinden, sobald ein Geschäftsmodell endet.

- Nach Server-Ende müssen nutzbare Wege bleiben.
- Möglich sein sollten Offline-Modus, LAN, private Server oder Archivlösung.
- Nach Store-Schließungen müssen gekaufte Inhalte weiter zugänglich bleiben.
- Updates, Patches und DLCs müssen langfristig gesichert werden.
- Aktivierungen dürfen nicht ersatzlos wegfallen.
- Digitale Käufe dürfen nicht schlechter geschützt sein als physische Käufe.

6. Modding, Emulatoren, Fanpatches und Archivierung

Nicht jede technische Nutzung ist Piraterie. Modding, Emulation, Fanpatches, Übersetzungen, Interoperabilität und Archivierung können wichtig für Erhalt, Barrierefreiheit, Forschung und Community sein.

- Mods dürfen nicht pauschal wie Angriffe behandelt werden.
- Emulatoren als Technik müssen geschützt bleiben.
- Fanpatches und Fanübersetzungen sollen erlaubt sein, wenn sie keine Originalwerke ersetzen.
- Archivierung und Forschung müssen rechtlich abgesichert werden.
- Community-Server sollen möglich sein, wenn offizielle Server abgeschaltet wurden.
- Barrierefreiheits-Mods sollen besonders geschützt werden.

7. Software, Schnittstellen und Interoperabilität

Software ist nicht nur Ware, sondern oft Infrastruktur. Deshalb braucht es faire Regeln für Schnittstellen, Datenzugang und Interoperabilität.

- Offene oder dokumentierte Schnittstellen müssen gefördert werden.
- Interoperabilität darf nicht künstlich blockiert werden.
- Datenexporte müssen möglich sein.
- Nutzer dürfen nicht vollständig in geschlossene Ökosysteme gezwungen werden.
- Lizenzbedingungen müssen fair und verständlich sein.
- Sicherheits- und Kompatibilitätsgründe dürfen nicht als Vorwand für unnötige Abschottung dienen.

8. Hardware, Reparatur und offene Standards

Bei Hardware müssen Schutzrechte mit Reparierbarkeit und langfristiger Nutzbarkeit zusammen gedacht werden.

- Ersatzteile müssen angemessen verfügbar sein.
- Reparaturanleitungen und technische Dokumentation müssen zugänglich sein.
- Reparatur darf nicht durch Softwarelocks blockiert werden.
- Offene Standards müssen bevorzugt werden.
- Plattformbindung darf Reparatur, Zubehör oder unabhängige Wartung nicht unnötig verhindern.
- Geräte dürfen nicht künstlich entwertet werden, nur weil ein Hersteller Support beendet.

9. Technische Qualität und Veröffentlichung

Gerade große Publisher und Plattformbetreiber müssen bei Veröffentlichungen höhere Verantwortung tragen.

- Technische Mindestqualität bei Release muss verbindlich werden.
- Unfertige Vollpreisspiele dürfen kein Normalzustand sein.
- Massive Bugs, schlechte Performance und kaputte Grundfunktionen müssen rechtliche Folgen haben können.
- DLCs, Season Passes oder Zusatzdienste dürfen keinen ausgelagerten Hauptinhalt ersetzen.
- Vorverkauf und Premium-Editionen müssen transparent sein.
- Käufer müssen erkennen können, was sie wirklich bekommen.

10. Abstufung nach Verantwortung

Nicht jeder Akteur kann gleich behandelt werden. Pflichten müssen nach Marktmacht, Kontrolle und Zumutbarkeit abgestuft werden.

- Plattformbetreiber tragen die stärkste Verantwortung.
- Große Publisher und Rechteinhaber tragen starke Verantwortung.
- Hardwarehersteller müssen Reparatur und langfristige Nutzbarkeit sichern.
- Kleine Entwickler und unabhängige Projekte dürfen nicht überfordert werden.
- Kleine Studios brauchen Schutz vor Patentdruck und juristischen Drohkulissen großer Unternehmen.
- Wer große Kontrolle über Kultur, Technik oder Infrastruktur hat, muss größere Pflichten tragen.

11. Fazit

Games, Software und Hardware sind nicht nur Ware. Sie sind Technik, Kultur, Infrastruktur und Erinnerung.

Schutzrechte sollten Kreativität, Qualität und Erhalt fördern. Sie dürfen nicht dazu dienen, neue Ideen, Nutzerrechte, Reparatur, Archivierung, Modding, Interoperabilität und faire Weiterentwicklung zu blockieren.

Wer maximale Kontrolle will, muss auch maximale Verantwortung übernehmen.

title: ffmpeg CHEAT SHEET
content:
### FASTEST CROP WITH MINIMAL QUALITY LOSS #####################################################
#
# out_w:out_h: Width and height of the cropped area.
# x:y: Top-left corner coordinates.
# -crf 18: Visually lossless quality (lower numbers = higher quality; 17–18 is standard for high fidelity).
# -c:a copy: Copies the audio without re-encoding it to save time

ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -vf "crop=out_w:out_h:x:y" -c:v libx264 -crf 18 -c:a copy output.mp4

### CONCATENATE 360 VIDEO FILES WITH 360 METADATA INTACT #######################################
# 
# even Google's AI got this wrong, sigh
# 
# first, create list of files to be concatenated, let's call it filelist.txt
# then type the filenames in it, like this:
# 
# file '/path/to/file1.mp4'
# file '/path/to/file2.mp4'
# file '/path/to/file3.mp4'
#
# then type the following command:
# the "-strict unofficial" will ensure that the 360 metadata will stay intact
 
ffmpeg -f concat -safe 0 -i filelist.txt -c copy  -strict unofficial  output.mp4
 
### CHANGE AUDIO VOLUME LEVEL BY MULTIPLIER OR DECIBEL & PRESERVE 360 VIDEO #####################

# change audio volume by 2x
ffmpeg -i input.mp4  -af "volume=2" -c:v copy output.mp4
 
# change audio volume into 5 dB
ffmpeg -i input.mp4  -af "volume=5db" -c:v copy output.mp4
 
# change audio volume by 2x AND preserve 360 video metadata
# otherwise the metadata will be stripped & video players will play it as 2D video
ffmpeg -i input.mp4  -af "volume=2" -c:v copy -strict unofficial output.mp4

### CONVERT TO WHATSAPP VIDEO FORMAT + CUT TO CERTAIN TIME #####################################
# this will result in whatsapp.mp4 which contains the contents of input.mp4 
# starting from minute 36 seconds 21 , with length of 10 seconds
# and it's compatible with WhatsApp
 
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -ss 00:36:21 -t 00:00:10 -vcodec libx264 -acodec aac whatsapp.mp4
 
# My helmet cam produces videos in a strange format, which is rejected by WhatsApp everytime
# This one-liner produces a video that will be able to be uploaded to WhatsApp

### RESIZE / SCALE  ffmpeg encoding with VAAPI / hardware acceleration #########################
# I got about 7x speed up + very low CPU usage 
# (so I'm able do other stuff while ffmpeg is busy doing the decoding/encoding)
 
# need this installed first 
apt-get install intel-media-va-driver-non-free
# if you keep getting the following error - that means you need to execute that command above:
#         Failed to create processing pipeline config: 12 (the requested VAProfile is not supported)
 
# let's go 
time ffmpeg -init_hw_device vaapi=foo:/dev/dri/renderD128 -hwaccel vaapi -hwaccel_output_format vaapi -hwaccel_device foo -i INPUT-720p.mp4 -filter_hw_device foo -vf 'format=nv12|vaapi,hwupload,scale_vaapi=iw/2:ih/2:mode=hq,fps=fps=25' -c:v h264_vaapi  -compression_level 1 -y -c:a copy OUTPUT-360p.mp4
 
# to find your vaapi device, type : ls  /dev/dri/* 
# see list of standard resolutions here : https://levvvel.com/169-resolutions/
# remove ",fps=fps=25" bit if you're not changing the fps rate

### FASTEST WAY TO CUT A VIDEO #############################################################
# this will result in output.mp4 which contains the contents of input.mp4 
# from minute 36 seconds 21 , with length of 10 seconds
 
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -ss 00:36:21 -t 00:00:10 -acodec copy -vcodec copy output.mp4

2 CommandBars("hyperlink Context Menu").Controls(10).Execute
